# CPA Validated
> Free, CPA-validated tax tools, validators, and reference guides. Founded April 2026. Every position backed by a specific IRC section or Treasury regulation. A tk.cpa resource.

## About
CPA Validated is a free, perpetual public resource built and personally validated by Timur Knyazev, CPA. Founded after Tax Day 2026 as a pro bono contribution from a licensed practitioner to the public. Every tool, validator, and guide is free, unconditioned, and cites primary legal authority.

## Practitioner Credentials
- NY CPA License #109025 (licensed 2012, active)
- FL CPA License #AC58472 (licensed 2022, active)
- IRS Enrolled Agent #93250 (enrolled 2009, active 2026, PTIN P00646638)
- Master of Science in Taxation - Zicklin School of Business, Baruch College (CUNY), 2011
- Big Four: Ernst & Young (EY) and PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC), ~10 years
- AICPA Member, CGMA, ACCA CertIFRS, TEI, CTP, IIA CFSA

## Tools & Validators

### Substantial Presence Test Calculator
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/spt-calculator.html
- Authority: IRC §7701(b)(3); Treas. Reg. 301.7701(b)-1; Form 1040-NR
- Purpose: Weighted 3-year day count, days to trigger SPT, maximum safe days remaining. Two input methods: manual entry or trip-by-trip date log.

### Closer Connection Exception Checker
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/closer-connection.html
- Authority: IRC §7701(b)(3)(B); Treas. Reg. 301.7701(b)-2; Form 8840
- Purpose: Tests Form 8840 eligibility. Evaluates all 11 IRS closer connection factors. Green Card disqualifier flagged automatically.

### FBAR and Form 8938 Threshold Checker
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/fbar-checker.html
- Authority: 31 USC 5314; 31 CFR 1010.350; IRC §6038D; FinCEN 114; Form 8938
- Purpose: Determines FinCEN 114 (FBAR) and Form 8938 (FATCA) filing requirements. All account types. All four Form 8938 thresholds.

### Safe Harbor Estimated Tax Calculator
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/safe-harbor.html
- Authority: IRC §6654; IRS Publication 505
- Purpose: Federal and all 50 states. 110% rule applied. Quarterly schedule with state-specific due dates.

### Form 5472 Filing Obligation Checker
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/form-5472.html
- Authority: IRC §6038A; IRC §6038C; TD 9796 (2016)
- Purpose: 25% foreign ownership threshold, foreign-owned DE rules, all 11 reportable transaction categories.

### Tax Estimator 2026 - Personal and Corporate
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/tax-estimator.html
- Authority: IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-32; IRC §1; IRC §11; OBBBA (P.L. 119-21)
- Purpose: 2026 personal and corporate tax estimates. All filing statuses, all 50 states, bracket-by-bracket breakdown.

### Puerto Rico Act 60 Tax Benefit Estimator
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/act60-estimator.html
- Authority: Act 60 (P.R. 2019); Act 38-2026; IRC §933; Treas. Reg. §1.937-1
- Purpose: PR Act 60 savings analysis. Eligibility checker. Dec 31, 2026 deadline for 0% rate.

### USVI EDC Tax Benefit Estimator
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/usvi-edc-estimator.html
- Authority: IRC §934; IRC §937; VI Code §713; USVIEDA
- Purpose: USVI EDC 90% tax credit. ~2.1% effective corporate rate. EDC vs. RTPark and PR Act 60 vs. USVI comparison.

### US Federal Tax Calendar 2026
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/tax-calendar.html
- Authority: IRC §6072; IRC §6654; IRC §6655; IRS Pub. 509; IRC §7503
- Purpose: Month-by-month calendar. W-2/1099-NEC Feb 2 (Jan 31=Saturday), 1065/1120-S Mar 16 (Mar 15=Sunday). Corporate and individual estimated tax. Key state deadlines.

### Real Estate Depreciation Calculator
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/depreciation-calculator.html
- Authority: IRC §168; Rev. Proc. 87-57; OBBBA (P.L. 119-21)
- Purpose: MACRS for residential (27.5yr), commercial (39yr), foreign ADS (40yr), QIP (15yr). Mid-month convention. Bonus depreciation for QIP: 40% (2026).

### Business Vehicle Depreciation Calculator
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/vehicle-depreciation.html
- Authority: IRC §168(k); IRC §179; IRC §280F; Rev. Proc. 2025-24
- Purpose: Section 179, bonus depreciation (40% 2026), MACRS 5-year. §280F luxury auto caps. SUVs, heavy trucks. Business use percentage slider.

### CFC / Form 5471 Obligation Checker
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/form-5471-checker.html
- Authority: IRC §957; IRC §951(b); IRC §6038; IRC §318/§958(b); TCJA §14214
- Purpose: 7-question validator. CFC status, all filing categories 1a-5b. TCJA downward attribution. $10,000 penalty flagged. Statute of limitations warning.

### Streamlined Filing Eligibility Checker
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/streamlined-checker.html
- Authority: IRM 4.26.16.6 (SFOP); IRM 4.26.16.7 (SDOP); 31 USC §5321
- Purpose: SDOP vs. SFOP eligibility. Non-willfulness test, IRS contact check, residency test (330 days). 0% penalty (SFOP) vs. 5% of highest balance (SDOP).

## Reference Guides

### One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA, P.L. 119-21) - The Biggest Provisions
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/obbba-guide.html
- Authority: P.L. 119-21; IRC §§24, 168(k), 168(n), 174A, 199A, 250, 951A, 1202, 2010(c)(3); Rev. Proc. 2025-28; Notice 2025-72
- Purpose: 18 verified provisions across individuals (SALT cap to $40K, $15M estate exemption, tip / overtime / senior / auto loan deductions, Trump Accounts), business (Section 174A R&E expensing, 100% bonus depreciation permanent, Section 168(n) qualified production property, Section 163(j) EBITDA, Section 199A QBI permanent, Section 1202 QSBS tiered exclusion, Section 179 expansion), and international (NCTI replaces GILTI, FDDEI replaces FDII, BEAT 10.5%). Effective dates and authority citations throughout.

### States with No Personal Income Tax (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/no-income-tax-states.html
- Authority: Tax Foundation 2026; State Tax Statutes; IRC §933
- Purpose: Nine states with no broad personal income tax. Washington capital gains tax exception. Sales and property tax context.

### Countries with No Personal Income Tax (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/no-income-tax-countries.html
- Authority: IRC §61; IRC §911; IRC §877A; 31 USC §5314
- Purpose: 19+ jurisdictions. Critical note for US persons: worldwide taxation regardless of residency. FEIE $126,500 (2026). Oman changing 2028.

### FBAR Penalties Explained (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/fbar-penalties.html
- Authority: 31 USC §5321; IRM 4.26.16; Bittner v. US (2023)
- Purpose: Willful vs. non-willful penalties. Bittner (2023): non-willful per form not per account. Streamlined, SDOP, SFOP, VDP explained.

### Pre-Immigration Tax Planning Checklist
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/pre-immigration-planning.html
- Authority: IRC §7701(b); IRC §1291-1298; IRC §951; IRC §877A
- Purpose: 9-step checklist before green card. Capital gains basis step-up, PFIC restructuring, foreign entity planning, dual-status year.

### Foreign Pension Reporting for US Persons
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/foreign-pension-reporting.html
- Authority: 31 USC §5314; IRC §6038D; IRC §402(b); Rev. Proc. 2020-17
- Purpose: Governmental DB vs. savings component distinction. Canada RRSP, Israel, Germany, UK, Russia. US-Russia treaty suspended August 2023.

### NCTI Explained (Formerly GILTI)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/gilti-explained.html
- Authority: IRC §951A (NCTI, formerly GILTI); IRC §250; IRC §957; IRC §960(d); IRC §962; OBBBA P.L. 119-21
- Purpose: NCTI - Net CFC Tested Income - is the post-OBBBA renaming of GILTI. Calculation, 12.6% effective corporate rate (40% Section 250 deduction), 90% foreign tax credit, elimination of QBAI shield, inclusion shareholder rule (any-day-during-year), §962 election, high-tax exclusion. Effective for tax years beginning after 12/31/2025.

### S-Corp vs. LLC Tax Comparison (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/scorp-vs-llc.html
- Authority: IRC §1361; IRC §1401; IRC §199A; Watson v. US (8th Cir. 2012)
- Purpose: SE tax savings, reasonable compensation, QBI interaction. $200K example. Break-even analysis.

### Form 3520: Foreign Gifts, Inheritances and Trusts
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/form-3520-guide.html
- Authority: IRC §6039F; IRC §6048; IRC §6677; IRC §679
- Purpose: 2026 thresholds ($100K individuals, $19,570 corporations). Penalties up to 35%. Aggregation rules. Trust reporting.

### IRC §1031 Like-Kind Exchange Guide (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/1031-exchange-guide.html
- Authority: IRC §1031; Treas. Reg. §1.1031(k)-1; Rev. Proc. 2000-37
- Purpose: 45-day identification, 180-day closing, boot, QI, reverse exchange. Real property only since TCJA 2018.

### Crypto & NFT Tax Guide (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/crypto-tax-guide.html
- Authority: IRS Notice 2014-21; Rev. Rul. 2023-14; IRC §1221; Form 8949
- Purpose: All taxable events. Mining, staking (Rev. Rul. 2023-14), DeFi. Wash sale does not apply. Form 1099-DA 2026 broker reporting.

### IRC §199A QBI Deduction Guide (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/qbi-deduction-guide.html
- Authority: IRC §199A; Treas. Reg. §1.199A-1; OBBBA (P.L. 119-21)
- Purpose: 20% deduction. OBBBA made permanent. W-2 wage limitation, SSTB phase-out, rental safe harbor, aggregation election.

### Net Investment Income Tax Guide (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/niit-guide.html
- Authority: IRC §1411; Treas. Reg. §1.1411-1; Form 8960
- Purpose: 3.8% surcharge on investment income above $200K/$250K. Real estate professional exception. Material participation. Crypto NIIT.

### Bonus Depreciation Guide (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/bonus-depreciation-guide.html
- Authority: IRC §168(k); IRC §168(n); IRC §179; IRC §163(j); IRC §199A; Treas. Reg. §1.168(k)-2; OBBBA (P.L. 119-21)
- Purpose: 100% bonus depreciation permanently restored under OBBBA for property acquired AND placed in service after 1/19/2025. Property under written binding contract before 1/20/2025 stays on TCJA phase-down (40% in 2025, 20% in 2026, 0% in 2027). Section 168(n) qualified production property (new under OBBBA, construction post-1/19/2025 to pre-1/1/2029, placed in service pre-1/1/2031). Section 179 expanded to $2.5M / $4M phaseout. Section 163(j) EBITDA permanent. State conformity (CA decoupled, NY decoupled).

### Meals &amp; Entertainment Deduction Guide (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/meals-and-entertainment-guide.html
- Authority: IRC §274; IRC §274(o); IRC §274(n)(1); IRC §274(e); Treas. Reg. §1.274-11; Treas. Reg. §1.274-12; Notice 2018-76; Notice 2021-25; T.D. 9925; OBBBA (P.L. 119-21)
- Purpose: 50% standard meal rule (§274(n)(1)). 0% entertainment disallowance (TCJA, permanent). NEW §274(o) effective 1/1/2026 disallowing 100% of convenience-of-employer meals (§119) and employer-operated eating facility expenses (§132(e)(2)) - OBBBA retained the sunset, did NOT delay or repeal. OBBBA added narrow exceptions: restaurants feeding own staff, fishing/fish-processing vessels under §274(n)(2)(C). Six 100% exceptions under §274(e). Documents expired §274(n)(2)(D) restaurant 100% (2021-2022, Notice 2021-25). Substantiation under §274(d). Chart-of-accounts recommendations.

### Charitable Contribution Deduction Guide (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/charitable-deduction-guide.html
- Authority: IRC §170; IRC §170(b)(1)(I) (new under OBBBA); IRC §170(b)(2)(A) (amended under OBBBA); IRC §170(p) (new under OBBBA); IRC §170(d)(1)(C); IRC §408(d)(8); OBBBA (P.L. 119-21)
- Purpose: OBBBA changes effective 1/1/2026. New 0.5% AGI floor for individual itemizers. New 1% taxable income floor for corporations. 60% AGI cash limit to public charities made permanent. 35% benefit cap on itemized deductions for top-bracket (37%) taxpayers. New $1,000 single / $2,000 joint above-the-line deduction for non-itemizers (DAFs and private non-operating foundations excluded). Pre-2026 carryforwards NOT subject to floor. Ordering rules under §170(d)(1)(C). AGI percentage limit table by donee/property type. Bunching strategy worked example. QCDs (up to $108K 2026 indexed) bypass the 0.5% floor.

### IRA &amp; Retirement Plan Contribution Limits (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/ira-basics-guide.html
- Authority: IRS Notice 2025-67; IRC §219; IRC §401(k); IRC §403(b); IRC §457(b); IRC §408 (IRA); IRC §408A (Roth IRA); IRC §408(p) (SIMPLE); IRC §415(b), §415(c); IRC §414(v) (catch-up); IRC §414(v)(7) (mandatory Roth catch-up, SECURE 2.0); IRC §223 (HSA); IRC §408(d)(8) (QCD); SECURE 2.0 Act of 2022; Treasury final regulations on SECURE 2.0 catch-up rules (issued September 16, 2025)
- Purpose: 2026 limits per IRS Notice 2025-67. 401(k)/403(b)/457(b) elective deferral $24,500. Standard catch-up age 50+ $8,000 (total $32,500). Super catch-up ages 60-63 $11,250 (total $35,750, REPLACES standard catch-up). IRA $7,500. IRA catch-up $1,100 (total $8,600). SIMPLE $17,000/$18,100. SEP up to $72,000 §415(c). HSA self-only $4,400, family $8,750, 55+ catch-up $1,000. §415(b) DB plan $290,000. Mandatory Roth catch-up effective 2026 for participants whose prior-year FICA wages exceeded $150K from sponsoring employer. IRA deductibility phase-outs (single covered $81-91K, joint covered $129-149K). Roth phase-outs (single $153-168K, MFJ $242-252K). Backdoor Roth pro-rata warning (Form 8606). RMD age 73 (75 in 2033). QCD (up to $108K 2026, indexed) bypasses OBBBA 0.5% AGI floor.

### US Expat Tax Filing Guide (2026)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/us-expat-tax-guide.html
- Authority: IRC §911; IRC §901; IRC §6012; 31 USC §5314
- Purpose: Who must file from abroad. FEIE $126,500. FTC vs FEIE. FBAR deadlines. State tax obligations. SE tax for self-employed expats.

## Special Pages

### Vetted Resources
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/vetted-resources.html
- Purpose: CPA-curated external resource directory. PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, OECD, EY, KPMG, official government tax authorities (IRS, HMRC, CRA, Israel ITA, German BZSt), Tax Foundation, Avalara, TaxJar. 18 resources across 4 categories.

### About CPA Validated
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/about.html
- Purpose: Mission statement. Five commitments: Always Free, Always Cited, Always Current, Always Honest, Always Accountable. Founded April 2026 after Tax Day as a pro bono public resource.


### IRS Penalty & Interest Calculator
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/penalty-calculator.html
- Authority: IRC §6651(a)(1) (FTF 5%/month); IRC §6651(a)(2) (FTP 0.5%/month); IRC §6601 (interest 8% Q2 2026); IRC §6662(a) (accuracy 20%)
- Purpose: Failure-to-file, failure-to-pay, accuracy-related penalty, underpayment interest. Concurrent FTF/FTP offset applied. Reasonable cause flag. Date-based calculation.

### Foreign Tax Credit Calculator
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/ftc-calculator.html
- Authority: IRC §901 (FTC); IRC §904 (limitation); IRC §911 (FEIE); Form 1116
- Purpose: FEIE vs. FTC comparison. IRC §904 per-basket limitation. Carryforward. Shows which strategy produces lower US tax by country type.

### W-4 Withholding Optimizer
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/w4-optimizer.html
- Authority: IRC §3402; IRC §6654; IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-32; Form W-4 (2026)
- Purpose: Target refund or balance due. Multiple jobs, spouse income, other income, credits. Produces exact W-4 Step 1-4 entries with per-period withholding amount.

### Corporate Extension Reference (C-Corp)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/corporate-extension.html
- Authority: IRC §6081; Form 7004; Treas. Reg. §1.6081-3; 59 state and local jurisdictions
- Purpose: Searchable/sortable C-Corp extension rules. Automatic vs. accepts federal vs. separate form. Verified March 2026. Includes local jurisdictions (NYC, Detroit, Philadelphia, SF, LA, Portland, Ohio RITA).

### Individual Extension Reference (Form 1040)
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/individual-extension.html
- Authority: IRC §6081(a); Form 4868; Treas. Reg. §1.6081-4; 59 jurisdictions
- Purpose: Form 1040 extension rules. Expat 2-month auto extension, Form 2350, FBAR auto-extension, military/combat zone. 9 no-PIT states identified.

### IRS Online Accounts Guide
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/irs-accounts-guide.html
- Authority: IRS.gov; ID.me; Form 2848 (Power of Attorney); Form 8821 (Tax Information Authorization); IRS Pub. 5367 (IP PIN); IRS Newsroom IR-2026-21
- Purpose: The three IRS online account types - Individual Online Account (sa.www4.irs.gov/ola), Business Tax Account (sa.www4.irs.gov/bola), and Tax Pro Account (sa.www4.irs.gov/taxpro). Direct sign-in URLs, what each does, who can register, ID.me identity verification, common error codes (EC 6000, 6001, 6101), e-Services, IRIS, EFTPS, Login.gov differences. Practical recommendations for individuals, businesses, and practitioners.

### IRS Payments Guide
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/irs-payments-guide.html
- Authority: irs.gov/payments; IRS Direct Pay; EFTPS.gov; IRS Same-Day Wire (with verified ABA 091036164 and beneficiary format from the IRS Same-Day Taxpayer Worksheet PDF); Foreign Electronic Payments; Form 1040-V (2025); Form 1040-ES (2026); IRS Pub. 4990 (Tax Type list); IRC §6151, §6611, §6621, §6651
- Purpose: All federal tax payment methods compared. IRS Direct Pay (free, individuals), EFTPS (free, individuals and businesses), same-day wire with full instructions including verified ABA 091036164, IRS SINGLE PAYOR TAX WIRES, 333 W. Pershing Rd Kansas City MO 64108, 5pm ET cutoff, full Tax Type Code table for common forms (1040, 1120, 941, 940, 1042, 2290), foreign wire for international taxpayers, pay by check (Form 1040-V) with how-to write the check correctly, debit/credit card, electronic funds withdrawal, pay by cash, online payment agreement (installment plans), and penalty / interest framework for late payments.

## Canadian Cross-Border (New Section)

tk.cpa is registered with the Canada Revenue Agency as a non-resident representative. Existing and prospective clients can authorize tk.cpa through CRA Represent a Client to retrieve T-slips (T4, T5, T4A, NR4), view Notices of Assessment, file T1 and T2 returns, and communicate with the CRA on the client's behalf.

### Canadian Tax Hub
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/canadian-tax-hub.html
- Authority: CRA Represent a Client; Income Tax Act (Canada); IRC §7701(b); US-Canada Tax Treaty
- Purpose: Landing page for cross-border content. Highlights the CRA non-resident representative credential. Lists the 5 main articles (treaty, residency, T1135, RRSP/RRIF, snowbirds). Plain-English explanation of how US and Canada differ on taxing citizens vs residents. Glossary of cross-border forms (T1, T2, T1135, NR4, NR73, NR301/302/303, Form 8833, Form 8840).

### The US-Canada Tax Treaty, Plainly
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/us-canada-tax-treaty.html
- Authority: US-Canada Tax Treaty (1980 as amended); Articles IV, X, XI, XII, XV, XVIII, XXIV, XXIX, XXIX-A; IRC §6114; Form 8833
- Purpose: Plain-English walkthrough of the treaty for cross-border individuals. Article IV residency tie-breaker (permanent home, center of vital interests, habitual abode, citizenship, mutual agreement), the savings clause and how it limits the treaty for US citizens, dividends 5%/15% (Article X), interest and royalties at 0% (Articles XI/XII), employment income (Article XV) and the 183-day exception, pensions and Social Security (Article XVIII), foreign tax credit (Article XXIV), Limitation on Benefits (Article XXIX-A), when Form 8833 is required.

### Are You a Canadian Tax Resident?
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/canadian-residency.html
- Authority: Income Tax Act (Canada) §250 (residency), §128.1 (change of residence), §2 (taxation of resident persons); CRA Income Tax Folio S5-F1-C1; Form NR73, T1243, T1161; US-Canada Treaty Article IV
- Purpose: Plain-English guide to Canadian residency. Factual residency (residential ties test - significant: dwelling, spouse, dependants; secondary: personal property, social/economic ties, driver's license, health card), deemed residency (183-day sojourner rule), departure tax under ITA §128.1 (deemed disposition at FMV on emigration date, with exclusions for Canadian real property, RRSP/RRIF/TFSA, business property), the NR73 question (file or not), step-up basis on entry, treaty tie-breaker for dual residents.

### T1135: Canada's Foreign Property Form
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/t1135-foreign-property.html
- Authority: Income Tax Act (Canada) §233.3, §162(7), §162(10), §162(10.1), §152(4)(b.2); Form T1135; CRA Foreign Income Verification Statement; Voluntary Disclosures Program; Douglas v. The King 2022 TCC 80; Horner v. The King 2025 TCC 23
- Purpose: Plain-English T1135 guide. $100,000 CAD cost threshold, what counts as specified foreign property (foreign cash, non-resident corp shares even held in Canadian brokerage, foreign real property, debts, intangibles, crypto), what's excluded (registered accounts, Canadian-bank foreign currency, Canadian mutual funds, personal-use vacation property), Part A (simplified $100K-$250K) vs Part B (detailed $250K+), penalties ($25/day to $2,500; gross negligence $500/month to $12K; demand failure $1,000/month to $24K; long-term 5% of cost), 3-year extended reassessment, FBAR comparison, voluntary disclosure path.

### RRSP and RRIF for US Persons
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/rrsp-rrif-us-persons.html
- Authority: Rev. Proc. 2014-55 (automatic election; eliminated Form 8891); IRC §§402(b), 408; US-Canada Treaty Article XVIII; Income Tax Act (Canada) §§146, 146.3, 207.5-207.7; Forms 1040, 1116, 8938, 3520/3520-A, 8621; FinCEN Form 114
- Purpose: Plain-English RRSP/RRIF guide for US persons. Automatic deferral election under Rev. Proc. 2014-55 (no more Form 8891), what's still required (FBAR, Form 8938 if thresholds met), withdrawal mechanics (Canadian withholding 25% lump sum / 15% periodic; US ordinary income on growth above basis with FTC offset), RRSP basis tracking, what to do when moving from Canada to the US (don't collapse), TFSA trap (not covered by treaty, foreign trust treatment, PFIC issues for non-Canadian funds inside).

### Snowbird Day Counting Done Right
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/cross-border-snowbirds.html
- Authority: IRC §7701(b); IRC §7701(b)(3)(B); Treas. Reg. §301.7701(b)-2; Form 8840 (Closer Connection); Form 1040-NR; Form 8833 (Treaty); IRC §6114; IRC §871(d); IRC §1445 (FIRPTA); IRC §2102; US-Canada Treaty Article IV; Income Tax Act (Canada) §250
- Purpose: Plain-English day counting guide for snowbirds. Substantial Presence Test math (current 100% + prior 1/3 + two years ago 1/6, threshold 183), the 122-day rule of thumb, Form 8840 Closer Connection Exception (under-183 hard cap, "closer connection" factors), treaty tie-breaker via Form 1040-NR + Form 8833 when over 183 days, days that don't count, Florida real estate triggers (rental income IRC §871(d) election, FIRPTA 15% withholding on sale, US estate tax with $60K exemption for non-residents).

### FIRPTA: Selling US Real Estate as a Foreign Person
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/firpta-foreign-sellers.html
- Authority: IRC §897 (FIRPTA - tax on gain by foreign persons from disposition of US real property interests); IRC §1445 (FIRPTA withholding); IRC §1446(f) (partnership interest withholding, on Form 8288 effective January 2026); Treas. Reg. §1.1445; Form 8288 (Rev. January 2026); Form 8288-A; Form 8288-B; Form 1040-NR; Form 1120-F; Form W-7 Exception 4; IRS FIRPTA Withholding guidance; EFTPS mandatory for FIRPTA payments effective September 30, 2025; IRC §6651, §7202; State withholding: California Rev. & Tax Code §18662 (3.33%), Hawaii HARPTA HRS §235-68 (7.25%), New York Tax Law §663
- Purpose: Plain-English FIRPTA guide. The three-tier withholding system (0% under $300K personal use, 10% $300K-$1M personal use, 15% default), how to define foreign person and US real property interest, Form 8288-B withholding certificate to reduce withholding upfront (90 days IRS processing), buyer's 20-day Form 8288 deadline, EFTPS mandatory for FIRPTA payments since 9/30/2025, refund process via Form 1040-NR or 1120-F, ITIN W-7 Exception 4 for sellers without SSN, January 2026 expansion to partnership interest transfers under IRC §1446(f) at 10% withholding, treaty considerations (Article XIII for US-Canada), state-level non-resident seller withholding regimes.

### Dual-Status Returns
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/dual-status-returns.html
- Authority: IRC §7701(b) (definition of US resident); IRC §7701(b)(4) (First-Year Choice election); IRC §6013(g) (election to treat non-resident spouse as US resident); IRC §6013(h) (residency change year MFJ election); IRC §63(c)(6)(B) (no standard deduction for dual-status); IRS Pub. 519; Form 1040; Form 1040-NR; Form 1116; Form 8854 (where expatriation applies); IRC §877A
- Purpose: Plain-English dual-status returns guide. When dual-status applies (residency starts mid-year on arrival or ends mid-year on departure), how to choose the base form (1040 if resident at year-end, 1040-NR if non-resident at year-end, with the other as attached statement, "Dual-Status Return" written across top), the three painful restrictions (no standard deduction, no MFJ default, no Head of Household), the §6013(g)/(h) joint election to overcome MFJ prohibition (subjects worldwide income to US tax), allocation of income across residency change date (wages by workdays, bonuses by earning period, investment income by payment date, capital gains by sale date), the First-Year Choice under §7701(b)(4) (early residency start), state-level part-year residency considerations, expatriation tax exposure for departing US citizens and long-term green card holders.

### ECI vs FDAP: How the US Taxes Non-Residents
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/eci-vs-fdap.html
- Authority: IRC §871 (tax on non-resident alien individuals); IRC §871(a) (FDAP - 30% on gross); IRC §871(b) (ECI - graduated rates on net); IRC §871(d) (election for real property income as ECI); IRC §871(h) (portfolio interest exemption); IRC §881 (foreign corporations parallel rules); IRC §864 (US trade or business; effectively connected); IRC §897 (FIRPTA real property gains as ECI); IRC §1441/§1442 (US payor withholding obligations); Form 1040-NR (Schedule NEC for FDAP, main form for ECI); Form W-8BEN (treaty rate claim); Form W-8ECI (ECI election); Form 1042-S; Form 8833; US-Canada Tax Treaty Articles VII, X, XI, XII
- Purpose: Plain-English guide to the two regimes for non-resident US tax. ECI (effectively connected income) at graduated rates on net basis - active US business, US wages, real property gains under FIRPTA, voluntary §871(d) election for real estate rental. FDAP (fixed determinable annual periodical) at 30% gross withholding by US payor at source - dividends, interest (mostly portfolio-exempt), royalties, rents, annuities, pensions. Side-by-side comparison table. The §871(d) election (almost always favorable for foreign rental landlords). Capital gains generally NOT subject to US tax for non-residents (with FIRPTA, ECI, 183-day rule, and §865 exceptions). Portfolio interest exemption under §871(h). Treaty effects - PE article (Article VII) for ECI, withholding rate reduction for FDAP via W-8BEN. State-level treatment.

### Form 8854 and the US Exit Tax
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/form-8854.html
- Authority: IRC §877A (mark-to-market expatriation tax for covered expatriates); §877A(g)(1) (covered expatriate definition); §877A(b) (deferral election); §877A(c) (specified tax-deferred accounts); §877A(d) (eligible deferred compensation items); §877A(f) (non-grantor trusts); IRC §2801 (succession tax on covered gifts and bequests at 40% rate); IRC §6039G (information reporting); IRC §877(e) (long-term resident, 8 of 15 tax years); Notice 2009-85; Form 8854; Form 708 (§2801 return); Form I-407; State Department fee schedule (renunciation reduced to $450 effective April 13, 2026)
- Purpose: Plain-English Form 8854 and US exit tax guide. Three covered expatriate tests: (1) net worth $2M, (2) average annual net income tax for prior 5 years exceeding $211,000 for 2026 expatriations (was $206,000 for 2025), (3) failure to certify 5-year tax compliance on Form 8854. Long-term resident definition (green card 8 of last 15 tax years). Mark-to-market regime: deemed sale of all worldwide property at FMV day before expatriation date; first $910,000 (2026) of net gain excluded. Special rules for: eligible deferred comp (30% withholding election or present-value inclusion at exit), specified tax-deferred accounts (IRAs/401(k)s fully includible day before expatriation), non-grantor trusts (30% withholding under §877A(f)). §877A(b) deferral election (interest, security required, treaty waiver). IRC §2801 succession tax: 40% on covered gifts and bequests from covered expatriate to US person, no time limit, no exclusion - Form 708 paid by recipient. Two narrow exceptions: dual citizens at birth, minor expatriates. Treaty tie-breaker for green card holders triggers expatriation event itself. Pre-expatriation cleanup via Streamlined Procedures or Relief Procedures for Certain Former Citizens.

### Section 1202 QSBS After OBBBA
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/section-1202-qsbs.html
- Authority: IRC §1202; §1202(a) (exclusion percentages including OBBBA tiered amendments §1202(a)(5)); §1202(b)(1) (per-issuer cap, $10M / $15M); §1202(b)(4) (aggregate gross asset threshold, $50M / $75M); §1202(c) (QSBS definition); §1202(e) (active business requirement); §1202(e)(3) (excluded industries); §1202(h)(2) (gift carryover holding period); IRC §1(h)(4) (28% rate on §1202 gain not excluded); §1045 (rollover); §351 / §368 (carryover rules blocking pre-OBBBA stock from getting new regime); OBBBA P.L. 119-21 §70431
- Purpose: Plain-English QSBS guide post-OBBBA (signed July 4, 2025). Hard split based on stock issuance date: pre-OBBBA stock retains old rules (5-year hold, $10M cap, $50M asset test); post-OBBBA stock (after July 4, 2025) gets tiered exclusion (50% at 3 years / 75% at 4 years / 100% at 5 years), $15M per-issuer cap (inflation-adjusted starting 2027), $75M aggregate gross asset test (inflation-adjusted starting 2027). Eligibility stack: (1) domestic C corporation, (2) original issuance acquisition, (3) gross asset test at issuance, (4) 80% active business test through holding period, (5) excluded industries (health, law, accounting, actuarial, performing arts, consulting, athletics, financial services, brokerage, banking, insurance, financing, leasing, investing, farming, mineral extraction, hotels/restaurants), (6) holding period, (7) per-issuer cap. 28% rate on non-excluded §1202 gain (effective 14% at 3-year hold, 7% at 4-year hold). Stacking strategies: gift to family (§1202(h)(2) carryover), non-grantor trusts as separate taxpayers each with own $15M cap. §1045 rollover for sales before 5-year mark. State conformity issues: California, NJ, PA do not conform.

### State Residency for HNW Relocators
- URL: https://cpavalidated.com/state-residency-hnw.html
- Authority: New York: NY Tax Law §605(b); 20 NYCRR §105.20; Matter of Gaied v. New York State Tax Appeals Tribunal, 22 N.Y.3d 592 (2014). California: Cal. Rev. & Tax. Code §17014; FTB Pub 1031; nine-month presumption §17014(c). NJ: N.J.S.A. 54A:1-2(m). Massachusetts: M.G.L. c. 62 §1; Article XLIV "Millionaire's Tax". Convenience-of-the-employer rule: NY 20 NYCRR §132.18; Conn. Gen. Stat. §12-711; 316 Neb. Admin. Code §22-003. Florida: Fla. Stat. §196.015 (homestead); Form DR-501. IRC §1202 California non-conformity. IRC §164(b)(6) (SALT cap)
- Purpose: Plain-English state residency change guide for HNW relocators ahead of major liquidity events. The two-prong test: (1) domicile - subjective intent + objective facts (one true permanent home), (2) statutory residency - 183+ days physically present AND maintain permanent place of abode (NY uses 11-month rule for permanent place of abode). The "Big Five" domicile factors: home, time, family, business, items near and dear. Required documentary acts: driver's license, voter registration, vehicle registration, homestead exemption, banking, professional services, will/estate planning, mailing address. The convenience of the employer rule (NY/CT/NE/PA-historical/DE) - relocators continuing to work for old-state employer can still be taxed by old state. California 9-month presumption. California QSBS non-conformity (up to 13.3% on full gain). Best states to move to (FL, TX, WY, NV, SD, TN, WA-with-cap-gains-caveat). Most aggressive auditor states (NY, CA, NJ, MA, IL, MN, CT). Worked example: California-resident QSBS founder Mark relocates to Naples FL 22 months before $20M acquisition. Documentation discipline: contemporaneous day-count calendar, receipts, "near and dear" inventory at move, multi-advisor coordination.

GitHub: https://github.com/tk-cpa/cpavalidated

## Disclaimer
All tools and guides are for informational purposes only and do not constitute legal or tax advice. Tax determinations are fact-specific. Consult a qualified tax professional before making any filing or planning decisions.
